mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in the unemployment rate above 4.5% signals deteriorating labor market conditions, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investor confidence weakens.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly expansion in real GDP (GDPC1) indicates strong aggregate demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through improved corporate earnings prospects.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns in GDP expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL) and declines in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) signal stagflation risks, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns in stagflation scenarios exceed 1.5% in 70% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects robust supply-side activity, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days through improved corporate margins.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns in industrial growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, rising federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS) combined with elevated inflation (CPIAUCSL) signal reduced equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns in tightening cycles exceed 1.5% in 70% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) indicates a tightening labor market, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days through wage-driven consumer spending.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns in payroll growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A surge in housing starts (HOUST) reflects strong demand for residential construction, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through sectoral momentum.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns in housing boom periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
